heading: Occupational Safety and Health Review Commission
label: 661 Occupational Safety and Health Review Commission

children.heading: Establishment; membership; appointment; Chairman
children.label: a Establishment; membership; appointment; Chairman
children.text: The Occupational Safety and Health Review Commission is hereby established. The Commission shall be composed of three members who shall be appointed by the President, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate, from among persons who by reason of training, education, or experience are qualified to carry out the functions of the Commission under this chapter. The President shall designate one of the members of the Commission to serve as Chairman.

children.heading: Terms of office; removal by President
children.label: b Terms of office; removal by President
children.text: The terms of members of the Commission shall be six years except that (1) the members of the Commission first taking office shall serve, as designated by the President at the time of appointment, one for a term of two years, one for a term of four years, and one for a term of six years, and (2) a vacancy caused by the death, resignation, or removal of a member prior to the expiration of the term for which he was appointed shall be filled only for the remainder of such unexpired term. A member of the Commission may be removed by the President for inefficiency, neglect of duty, or malfeasance in office.

children.heading: Principal office; hearings or other proceedings at other places
children.label: d Principal office; hearings or other proceedings at other places
children.text: The principal office of the Commission shall be in the District of Columbia. Whenever the Commission deems that the convenience of the public or of the parties may be promoted, or delay or expense may be minimized, it may hold hearings or conduct other proceedings at any other place.

children.heading: Functions and duties of Chairman; appointment and compensation of administrative law judges and other employees
children.label: e Functions and duties of Chairman; appointment and compensation of administrative law judges and other employees
children.text: The Chairman shall be responsible on behalf of the Commission for the administrative operations of the Commission and shall appoint such administrative law judges and other employees as he deems necessary to assist in the performance of the Commission’s functions and to fix their compensation in accordance with the provisions of chapter 51 and subchapter III of chapter 53 of title 5 relating to classification and General Schedule pay rates: , That assignment, removal and compensation of administrative law judges shall be in accordance with sections 3105, 3344, 5372, and 7521 of title 5.Provided

children.heading: Quorum; official action
children.label: f Quorum; official action
children.text: For the purpose of carrying out its functions under this chapter, two members of the Commission shall constitute a quorum and official action can be taken only on the affirmative vote of at least two members.

children.heading: Hearings and records open to public; promulgation of rules; applicability of Federal Rules of Civil Procedure
children.label: g Hearings and records open to public; promulgation of rules; applicability of Federal Rules of Civil Procedure
children.text: Every official act of the Commission shall be entered of record, and its hearings and records shall be open to the public. The Commission is authorized to make such rules as are necessary for the orderly transaction of its proceedings. Unless the Commission has adopted a different rule, its proceedings shall be in accordance with the Federal Rules of Civil Procedure.

children.heading: Depositions and production of documentary evidence; fees
children.label: h Depositions and production of documentary evidence; fees
children.text: The Commission may order testimony to be taken by deposition in any proceeding pending before it at any state of such proceeding. Any person may be compelled to appear and depose, and to produce books, papers, or documents, in the same manner as witnesses may be compelled to appear and testify and produce like documentary evidence before the Commission. Witnesses whose depositions are taken under this subsection, and the persons taking such depositions, shall be entitled to the same fees as are paid for like services in the courts of the United States.

children.heading: Investigatory powers
children.label: i Investigatory powers
children.text: For the purpose of any proceeding before the Commission, the provisions of are hereby made applicable to the jurisdiction and powers of the Commission.section 161 of this title

children.heading: Administrative law judges; determinations; report as final order of Commission
children.label: j Administrative law judges; determinations; report as final order of Commission
children.text: A administrative law judge appointed by the Commission shall hear, and make a determination upon, any proceeding instituted before the Commission and any motion in connection therewith, assigned to such administrative law judge by the Chairman of the Commission, and shall make a report of any such determination which constitutes his final disposition of the proceedings. The report of the administrative law judge shall become the final order of the Commission within thirty days after such report by the administrative law judge, unless within such period any Commission member has directed that such report shall be reviewed by the Commission.1So in original. Probably should be “An”.1

children.heading: Appointment and compensation of administrative law judges
children.label: k Appointment and compensation of administrative law judges
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in this chapter, the administrative law judges shall be subject to the laws governing employees in the classified civil service, except that appointments shall be made without regard to . Each administrative law judge shall receive compensation at a rate not less than that prescribed for GS–16 under .section 5108 of title 5section 5332 of title 5
